_comment: darkmux profile registry — example. Four general-purpose reference profiles in increasing capacity order: fast → balanced → deep → gpt-oss, plus three review-pipeline-dedicated profiles (devstral, qwen3.6-27b-review, judge-35b-moe — see `_review_staffing_comment` below) whose models the review resourcing resolver scores per seat, plus one hosted endpoint example (hosted-frontier). A profile's `models[]` are WORKER models (no `role` field — #590); the machine's standing utility/compactor model lives once in the top-level `internal.utility` binding, not per-profile. `default_model` (optional) names a profile's default worker when it lists more than one; with a single model it's implicit. `n_ctx` is required for LOCAL models (it is the load parameter) and omitted for endpoint-bearing models (#1282). Replace the model ids with what you have downloaded in LMStudio (run `lms ls`).

fast.description: Single-turn tasks. Slim worker — fastest dispatch, lowest RAM.

balanced.description: Mid-range tasks. Predictable behavior across mixed workloads; compaction runs on the machine's internal.utility model.

deep.description: Long agentic tasks. Maximum worker context for fewer compactions.

gpt-oss.description: OpenAI gpt-oss-120b (MXFP4 quantization) for Apple Silicon — worker at 100K. Requires OpenClaw >= v2026.5.9-beta.1 (harmony format / tool-payload fix).

devstral.description: Review pipeline — probe seat, plain draws. Dense wide-instruct model, no reasoning trace.

qwen3.6-27b-review.description: Review pipeline — probe seat, bundle-scoped draws. A second, distinct dense wide-instruct model so the probe seat isn't a single point of view.

judge-35b-moe.description: Review pipeline — judge seat. MoE reasoner weighing the probe seat's findings; deliberately its own profile/model so it never doubles as a probe.

hosted-frontier.description: Hosted (endpoint-bearing) model — declares NO n_ctx: the provider owns the context window, so there is no local load parameter to invent (#1282). No `auth` block here either: a placeholder credential source would make a fresh `darkmux init` fail doctor's credentials check spuriously. When your endpoint needs credentials, add an `auth` block naming a credential SOURCE — either `"auth": { "type": "api-key", "keychain": "<item-name>" }` (a macOS Keychain ITEM name, never the secret itself) or `"auth": { "type": "api-key", "key_env": "AZURE_FINHEROGPT_KEY" }` (#1312 — the NAME of an env var holding the key, for ANY provider: `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `AZURE_FINHEROGPT_KEY`, anything). You can declare BOTH — `"auth": { "type": "api-key", "keychain": "darkmux-azure-finherogpt", "key_env": "AZURE_FINHEROGPT_KEY" }` — and the env var WINS when it is present in the environment: `security` is never read, so a headless CI runner (export the var from its secret store) never touches a lockable login keychain. Neither `keychain` nor `key_env` ever holds the secret VALUE; only names. Replace the URL/deployment with your own, or delete this profile if you only run local models.

_review_staffing_comment: Review staffing is DERIVED via the role->profile flip (#1475), not declared. `darkmux mission launch review` resolves each of the five review roles (review-probe-high / -mid / -low, review-judge, review-verify) INDEPENDENTLY: a per-run launch override (`--param <role>=<profile>`) wins, else the machine-local `role_profiles` map in config.json (`darkmux config set role_profiles.<role> <profile>`), else `default_profile` (the fresh-user floor -- a bare machine runs every seat on one model). Recall diversity is role-borne: the three distinct probe roles bind to three (possibly different) profiles. Bind the map for the durable heterogeneous crew (e.g. review-probe-high -> a devstral profile, review-judge -> a judge-35b-moe profile); override per run to escalate one seat (`--param review-judge=<denser-profile>`) without editing config.json. A leftover `crews` key from a pre-2.0 file is harmless residue (`darkmux doctor` notes it).
